description: This false-colour satellite image map, acquired by SPOT-5 (2.5 m resolution) in 2011-07, depicts an agricultural and urban landscape in Bozbulut, Turkey. The image uses a 1/2/3 band combination, where healthy vegetation, primarily agricultural fields, appears in shades of red and pink. Bare soil or urban built-up areas are shown in lighter tones, such as the town visible on the left side of the map. A winding river, depicted in dark teal/blue, traverses the upper right and lower right portions of the map. The central feature is a prominent gravel pit area containing multiple water bodies, outlined in black and filled with light blue and dark blue, consistent with "Water bodies related to active quarries". These water bodies are situated within the broader agricultural matrix. No scale bar, compass, or explicit legend is visible within the image frame.
